groups.name: Graduation Requirements
groups.description: University-wide minimums per UC Berkeley registrar.

requirements.name: Senior Residence (24 units)
requirements.description: At least 24 units must be completed in residence at Berkeley after senior standing (90 units).

requirements.name: Entry Level Writing (ELW)
requirements.description: Satisfied by passing R&C A, AP English Lang, or other accepted means.

rule.req: Entry Level Writing & Reading Composition A

requirements.name: American History & Institutions
requirements.description: One course covering both American History AND American Institutions.

groups.name: Essential Skills
groups.description: All L&S students must complete Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language

groups.name: Seven-Course Breadth
groups.description: Complete one course in each of the seven breadth areas. Auto-verified against each course's official Berkeley breadth designation.

groups.name: American Cultures (AC)
groups.description: All Berkeley undergraduates must complete one American Cultures course

requirements.name: American Cultures requirement
requirements.description: One course designated American Cultures (AC). Auto-verified from Berkeley's official breadth tag.

requirements.name: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs
Engineering Statistics, Quality Control, and Forecasting
Probability and Risk Analysis for Engineers
Introduction to Machine Learning and Data Analytics
Three concentration electives from approved list
Three lower division History of Art courses (one Western, one non-Western)
Area B: Arts of Africa, Oceania, Americas
Social, Political, and Ethical Environment of Business

programs.name: Bioengineering/Materials Science and Engineering Joint Major
Chemical Engineering / Materials Science & Engineering Joint Major

requirements.name: Dynamics and Control of Chemical Processes

programs.name: Chemical Engineering / Nuclear Engineering Joint Major

requirements.name: Process Design or Nuclear Reactor Theory
Dynamics and Control of Chemical Processes
Radiation Detection and Nuclear Instrumentation Laboratory
Principles and Techniques of Data Science
Human Contexts and Ethics of Data

groups.name: Domain Emphasis
groups.description: 5 upper-division courses in a chosen domain emphasis area (Economics, Social Science, Cognitive Science, Life Science, etc.). Approved list spans multiple departments including ECON, SOCIOL, PSYCH, COGSCI, PBHLTH, POLSCI, LEGALST, GPP, ESPM, ENVECON, and more.

programs.name: EECS / Materials Science & Engineering Joint Major

requirements.name: Designing Information Devices and Systems I
Designing Information Devices and Systems I
Designing Information Devices and Systems II

programs.name: East Asian Religion, Thought and Culture

requirements.name: Two semesters of East Asian language
Tools and Methods in East Asian Philosophy and Religion
Five courses in Buddhist, Chinese, Japanese, Tibetan religious/philosophical subjects
Two breadth courses in related disciplines
Seminar: Russian, East European, and Eurasian Cultures
Upper Division Economics Electives
requirements.description: 5 upper-div electives. Approved cross-division (CYPLAN, ENVECON, HISTORY, LEGALST, UGBA, etc.) capped at 2 per Berkeley policy. Source: BtLL reference reqs.

requirements.name: Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs
Designing Information Devices and Systems I
Designing Information Devices and Systems II
Minimum 20 units upper division EECS courses
Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs
Designing Information Devices and Systems I
Designing Information Devices and Systems II
Minimum 20 units upper division EECS courses
Concentration: Biological, Physical, or Social Science (15+ UD units)
One of: Film Aesthetics, Digital Media, Television Studies
Minimum 16 units of upper division electives

programs.name: Industrial Engineering & Operations Research & Business Administration

requirements.name: Group C: Structure, Function, Human Health
Introduction to Japanese Literature and Media
Social and Psychological Factors in Design

programs.name: Materials Science & Engineering / Mechanical Engineering Joint Major
Materials Science & Engineering / Nuclear Engineering Joint Major
Materials Science & Engineering and Business Administration

requirements.name: Rausser College of Natural Resources electives (15 units)
Rausser College of Natural Resources electives (15 units)
Introduction to Culture and Natural Resource Management
Concentration: U.S. Policy, Global Environmental Politics, or Justice and Sustainability (24 units)
Two additional lower division Theater courses
Students must complete 2 lower division courses.

groups.name: Lower Division
groups.description: No P grades allowed

groups.name: Upper Division
groups.description: A course with the identical course number may only be counted twice toward the minor and only if they have different topics. Only two courses may be repeated this way.

requirements.name: Students must complete 5 upper division courses.

groups.name: Upper Division
groups.description: Theater 40 and Theater 45 may be used/repeated for the Dance Technique requirement. The same course cannot be used to fulfill both the lower division and upper division requirement.

groups.name: Upper Division
groups.description: The Choroegraphy requirement can be fulfilled with THEATER 146A for 1 unit only if cast as a Dancer. To enroll as a Choreographer students must first complete THEATER 144 or THEATER 148 and then enroll in THEATER 146A for 3 units.

requirements.name: Principles and Techniques of Data Science
Human Contexts and Ethics of Data

groups.name: Domain Emphasis
groups.description: Two courses from Domain Emphasis approved list

requirements.name: Designing Information Devices and Systems I
Designing Information Devices and Systems II
Three upper-division EE, CS, or EECS courses (excluding 195, H196, 197, 198, 199; INFO 159 and STAT/DATA/CS C100 are the only non-CS/EE/EECS courses allowed)

groups.name: General Guidelines
groups.description: General Guidelines ​All minors must be declared no later than one semester before a student's Expected Graduation Term (EGT). If the semester before EGT is fall or spring, the deadline is the last day of RRR week. If the semester before EGT is summer, the deadline is the final Friday of Summer Sessi...

groups.name: Electives
groups.description: Students can not use both INDENG 172 and STAT 134 to fulfill this requirement.

groups.name: Upper Division
groups.description: Students must complete each course with a letter grade of C- or higher. At least three upper division courses must be ENERES.

groups.name: Upper Division Core
groups.description: Students must complete at least one course from two different areas for a total of six units: Natural Science Social Sciences Food and Community Health

groups.name: Upper Division Core
groups.description: SOCIOL 139F: Social Problems of the Food Industry may only fulfill this requirement with advisor approval.

groups.name: Upper Division Core
groups.description: PBHLTH 196: Global Nutrition may only fulfill this requirement with advisor approval.

groups.name: Upper Division Electives
groups.description: Choose two courses from the categories below. One elective must be from the category not chosen for a core course. Core course options not taken to fulfill the core requirement can be counted toward the elective requirement.

groups.name: Upper Division Electives
groups.description: ESPM 150: Green Water, Brown Ground, and Global Food Security may only fulfill this requirement with advisor approval.

groups.name: Upper Division Electives
groups.description: HISTORY 2: Foodways: A Global History and IAS 150: Climate Change and Agriculture in Latin America may only fulfill this requirement with advisor approval.

groups.name: Field Study
groups.description: Students must see advisor for enrollment information.

groups.name: Foundation
groups.description: Verify with the Geography Department: students complete one course from each of 3 different research areas. GEOG 198/199 may substitute with advisor approval.

groups.name: Research Areas (3 of 5)
groups.description: Choose one course from each of 3 different research areas (A through E).

groups.name: Lower Division
groups.description: Courses may be repeated with different class iterations.

groups.name: Lower Division Prerequisites
groups.description: Students must complete either MATH 1A and 1B or MATH 16A and 16B.

groups.name: Upper Division Electives
groups.description: Students can not use both CIVENG 122N and 123N to satisfy this requirement

groups.name: Upper Division Electives
groups.description: Students can not use both CIVENG 122N and 123N to satisfy this requirement.

groups.name: Campus-Wide Requirements
groups.description: Five requirements every Berkeley undergraduate must satisfy regardless of major or college.

requirements.name: Minimum 120 Units to Graduate
requirements.description: A baccalaureate degree requires at least 120 semester units of coursework.

requirements.name: Senior Residence (24 units)
requirements.description: After reaching senior standing (90 units), at least 24 of the remaining units must be completed in residence at Berkeley.

requirements.name: Entry Level Writing Requirement (ELWR)
requirements.description: Must be satisfied before completing 30 units. Met by AP English Lang/Lit, IB English HL, SAT/ACT essay scores, or by passing College Writing R1A or English R1A.

rule.req: Entry Level Writing & Reading Composition A

requirements.name: American History & Institutions (AH&I)
requirements.description: One course covering both American History AND American Institutions. May also be satisfied by an approved high-school course taken in the U.S.

requirements.name: American Cultures (AC)
requirements.description: One course officially designated AC by Berkeley's American Cultures Center. Required of every undergraduate regardless of college.

groups.name: Six Humanities & Social Studies Courses
groups.description: CoE undergraduates must complete 6 H/SS courses (≥18 units total). Specific structural rules: 2 courses upper-division, 2 in the same series/department, 2 covering different breadth areas. This planner counts the 6 minimum; consult your advisor for the structural rules.

groups.name: UBA Breadth Requirements
groups.description: UBA students complete 7 breadth areas alongside Haas core courses. Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language are pre-Haas requirements.

groups.name: Essential Skills
groups.description: CNR-specific essentials in addition to UC Berkeley's campus-wide requirements.

groups.name: CNR Breadth Areas (7)
groups.description: CNR undergraduates complete one course in each of seven breadth areas, mirroring the L&S structure but managed by the CNR Office of Instruction & Student Affairs.

groups.name: CED Breadth Areas
groups.description: CED follows the seven L&S breadth areas with the addition of design-focused electives managed by the college.

groups.name: Humanities & Social Sciences (6 courses)
groups.description: CoC undergrads take 6 H/SS courses spanning the L&S breadth areas. Same structural rules as CoE H/SS apply.

groups.name: Core
groups.description: Sutardja Center for Entrepreneurship & Technology (CET) certificate: complete the Berkeley Method of Entrepreneurship core + 3 SCET courses spanning ≥9 units. Verify the latest list at scet.berkeley.edu/students/certificates.

groups.name: Design Innovation Coursework
groups.description: Berkeley Design Innovation Certificate (BDI). Four courses in design thinking + studio + capstone. Track managed by SCET / Jacobs Institute.

groups.name: Changemaker Coursework
groups.description: Berkeley Changemaker Certificate (L&S): three Changemaker-designated courses + a high-impact final project. Many Changemaker courses are cross-listed; verify designation each semester at changemaker.berkeley.edu.

requirements.name: Berkeley Changemaker Foundation Course
requirements.description: L&S 1B "Berkeley Changemaker" or another foundation Changemaker course.

groups.name: Public Health Foundations
groups.description: School of Public Health undergraduate certificate. 5 courses covering the breadth of public health.

requirements.name: Introduction to Public Health (PB HLTH 150A or W150A)

groups.name: Bioethics Coursework
groups.description: Interdisciplinary certificate co-sponsored by the philosophy and biology departments. Covers ethics in biomedical research, biotechnology, and clinical practice.

groups.name: Engineering Leadership Coursework
groups.description: Fung Institute for Engineering Leadership undergraduate professional certificate. Five courses covering leadership, communication, project management, and engineering ethics.

groups.name: Sustainability Coursework
groups.description: Cross-college sustainability certificate. Five courses spanning environmental science, policy, and design.

programs.name: Blum Center · Global Poverty & Practice (GPP) Certificate

groups.name: GPP Coursework + Practice
groups.description: Global Poverty & Practice undergraduate program (Blum Center). 5 courses + a 240-hour Practice Experience. Available as a minor; this entry tracks the coursework only.

groups.name: Human Rights Coursework
groups.description: Cross-college interdisciplinary certificate run through the Human Rights Center. Five courses spanning law, history, and political science.

groups.name: Cal Teach Coursework
groups.description: Cal Teach: prepares Berkeley undergrads in STEM majors to become K-12 math or science teachers. Five courses + classroom field placements.

requirements.name: Intro to Math & Science Teaching (UGIS / EDUC 130)

groups.name: Demography Coursework
groups.description: Department of Demography certificate. Five courses spanning population science, statistics, and policy.

groups.name: Computational Economics Coursework
groups.description: Cross-listed certificate for econ majors who want a computational / data-science overlay. Five courses combining economics and CS / statistics.

groups.name: Pre-Health Course Prerequisites
groups.description: Pre-Med / Pre-Health is a track, not a major — students choose any major. These are the medical-school prerequisites the Career Center recommends. Allopathic (MD), osteopathic (DO), and PA programs all accept these. Verify your specific target schools at careers.berkeley.edu/pre-health.

groups.name: Pre-Law Skill Development
groups.description: Pre-Law is a track, not a major — law schools accept any undergraduate major. The Career Center recommends developing strong reading, writing, analytical, and oral-communication skills. These courses build the foundations the LSAT and 1L year reward.

groups.name: Language Prerequisites
groups.description: East Asian Languages & Cultures, Japanese track. Verify with EALC undergraduate advisor at ealc.berkeley.edu.

groups.name: Language Sequence
groups.description: Slavic Languages & Literatures: choose Russian, Polish, Czech, BCS (Bosnian/Croatian/Serbian), or Bulgarian as your primary language. Verify with department.

groups.name: Minor Coursework (5 upper-division)
groups.description: Five upper-division Slavic / E.European courses, ≥3 with substantive language content. Verify with Slavic department.

groups.name: LGBT Studies Coursework (5 courses)
groups.description: LGBT Studies minor administered through the Gender & Women's Studies department. Five courses, ≥3 upper-division. Cross-listed courses count.

groups.name: Prerequisites
groups.description: English major. Verify catalog year requirements at english.berkeley.edu.

groups.name: Lower Division (1)
groups.description: History major. Verify field of concentration at history.berkeley.edu/undergraduate.

requirements.name: Approved upper-div Classics, Latin, Greek, Ancient History

groups.name: Prerequisites
groups.description: MELC major (formerly Near Eastern Studies). Students concentrate in Arabic, Hebrew, Persian, Turkish, or another approved Middle Eastern language.

programs.name: Asian American and Asian Diaspora Studies
Asian American and Asian Diaspora Studies

groups.name: Eligibility
groups.description: L&S College Honors: ≥3.65 cumulative GPA, ≥3.65 GPA in major, completion of an honors thesis or research project. GPA + thesis approval are tracked in CalCentral, not here.

rule.req: Entry Level Writing & Reading Composition A

groups.name: Honors-level coursework
groups.description: Departments offering Honors tracks list specific honors-section courses (e.g. ENGLISH H195, ECON H196, MATH H110). The 4+ courses are typically in your major; check your department.

requirements.name: Applied math cluster — choose 1 from area A
Applied math cluster — choose 1 from area B

programs.name: Cal Corps Public Service Internship Certificate

requirements.name: Data 8 / DATA C8 — Foundations of DS
